title: The Holy Quran
description: The central religious text of Islam, believed by Muslims to be the literal word of God as revealed to the Prophet Muhammad. It covers guidance on faith, morality, law, and spirituality, and is renowned for its linguistic beauty, depth of meaning, and influence on Islamic civilization.

title: The Secret of Secrets
author: Dan Brown
description: A fast-paced thriller filled with cryptic codes, ancient secrets, and hidden societies. The story follows a protagonist unraveling a conspiracy that blends science, religion, and history, keeping readers on edge with unexpected twists.

title: Much Too Vulgar
author: Viggy Parr Hampton
description: A provocative horror novel that blends dark humor with disturbing imagery. It explores taboo themes through a satirical lens, challenging conventional boundaries while delivering a chilling and unsettling reading experience.

title: Samyabadi
author: Kazi Nazrul Islam
description: A powerful poetic work that champions equality, justice, and resistance against oppression. Through passionate verses, Nazrul calls for unity beyond class, religion, and caste, making it a timeless voice of rebellion and humanity.

title: Choritrohin
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: A classic Bengali novel that deeply examines social stigma, morality, and human relationships. It portrays complex characters navigating love, betrayal, and societal judgment, making it one of the most thought-provoking works in Bengali literature.

title: Himu Remande
author: Humayun Ahmed
description: Part of the iconic Himu series, this novel places the eccentric, philosophical character Himu in police custody. Despite the situation, Himu continues to challenge logic, authority, and societal norms with his mysterious worldview.

title: Pather Panchali
author: Bibhutibhushan Bandyopadhyay
description: A deeply emotional story of rural Bengal, following the childhood of Apu and his sister Durga. It beautifully captures poverty, dreams, family bonds, and the simplicity of village life with poetic realism.

title: French Revolution: A History From Beginning to End
author: Hourly History
description: A concise yet informative account of the French Revolution, exploring its causes, major events, and long-term impact on politics and society. Ideal for readers seeking a clear understanding of one of history’s most transformative periods.

title: Bhagavad Gita As It Is
description: A sacred Hindu scripture presented as a dialogue between Prince Arjuna and Lord Krishna. It addresses duty, righteousness, and the nature of life, offering timeless spiritual wisdom and philosophical insight.

title: The Big Book of Dad Jokes
author: Joe Kerz
description: A collection of hilariously cheesy jokes, puns, and one-liners that are so bad, they’re good. Perfect for light-hearted entertainment, family fun, or mastering the art of the classic ‘dad joke.’

title: The Dying Detective
author: Leif GW Persson
description: A gripping crime novel about a retired detective determined to solve a decades-old case while on his deathbed. It combines suspense, introspection, and meticulous investigation.

title: Die Bibel
description: The German edition of the Bible, a foundational text of Christianity. It contains the Old and New Testaments, offering teachings, stories, and spiritual guidance that have shaped Western culture and faith traditions.

title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
author: J.K. Rowling
description: In this third installment of the Harry Potter series, Harry returns to Hogwarts while a dangerous prisoner escapes Azkaban prison. The story deepens the magical world with darker themes, emotional revelations, and unforgettable twists.

title: Sophie's World
author: Jostein Gaarder
description: A unique novel that introduces readers to the history of Western philosophy through the story of a young girl receiving mysterious lessons. It blends fiction with deep philosophical exploration in an accessible way.

title: Gitanjali
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: A celebrated collection of poems that explores spirituality, nature, and human connection with the divine. Known for its lyrical beauty, it earned Tagore the Nobel Prize in Literature and remains a timeless masterpiece.
